input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup and listens for changes automatically — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (convenience helper)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property (generic version)
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts automatically** | Switches when the user toggles the OS theme — no manual subscription required |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define `Color` resources and reference them: `{AppThemeBinding Light={StaticResource LightBg}, Dark={StaticResource DarkBg}}` |
| **Override system theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a specific theme |
| **Detect current theme** | Read `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`) |
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to make a ContentPage theme-aware. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code change to an existing repo. I'll provide a concise, practical example.
